warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose: Fukui reactivity indices for formaldehyde were calculated using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard') in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The input geometry (`formaldehyde.xyz`) was fixed; no optimization was performed for this calculation. Mulliken charges were used (PySCF scheme). The Fukui indices f+ and f− were computed for electrophilic and nucleophilic attacks. Results: the carbon atom (C, atom 2) is most electrophilic with f+ ≈ 0.70839, while the oxygen atom (O, atom 1) is most nucleophilic with f− ≈ 0.46742. Trustworthy status per integrity checks. The residue sum warnings indicate f+ and f− totals matched conservation expectations (~1.0). Live files: plot visualization saved to `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/argo_gpt-4o/20260704-052524_formaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`, JSON results logged at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp6o9ukcpv/live.json`. Computational provenance: `cli.py fukui ... --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp`.
